reference.title: CLI options reference
reference.shortdesc: Complete reference for all md-validate commands and options. Use this page to look up the syntax, default values, and effect of every command-line flag.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Complete reference for all md-validate command-line options, subcommands, and flags with examples.

section.title: Synopsis
section.codeblock:
md-validate [--verbose] [--quiet] COMMAND [OPTIONS]
Commands: validate Validate a Markdown file or directory repl Start the interactive REPL

section.title: Global options
section.p: These options apply to all subcommands and must appear before the subcommand name.

strow.stentry: Enable DEBUG-level logging. Use this when diagnosing unexpected results or rule failures.
Suppress all non-error output. Only the exit code indicates pass or fail. Useful in CI pipelines that parse exit codes only.

section.title: validate — Validate a file or directory

strow.stentry: Path to a .md file or a directory. When a directory is provided, all .md files are validated recursively.
Path to the JSON rule set file.
Directory where report files are written. One file is created per validated document. The directory is created if it does not exist.
Report format. One of: text (human-readable terminal output), json (machine-readable JSON object), csv (one row per rule).
Controls whether workflow chains are executed after individual rule evaluation. Use --no-workflows to evaluate rules only.

section.title: repl — Start the interactive REPL
section.p: Starts the interactive REPL for rule development and debugging. No options are required. See the REPL commands reference for the list of REPL commands.

section.title: Examples
section.codeblock:
# Validate a single file with text output md-validate validate article.md --rules rules/tutorial.json
# Validate a directory and save JSON reports md-validate validate docs/ --rules rules/tutorial.json \ --format json --output reports/
# Validate quietly (exit code only, no output) md-validate --quiet validate docs/ --rules rules/tutorial.json
# Validate with debug logging md-validate --verbose validate article.md --rules rules/tutorial.json
